$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for v4's CharactersRepository.update (Phase-2,
the store-backed capstone sub-unit 4a — the update vault integration). One
committed file drives BOTH sides:
- the v4 oracle (build-characters-update-fixture.ts + characters-update.ts)
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness characters_update tier-2 test).
WHAT IT PORTS: applyDocumentStoreWriteOverlay (the managed-field write router)
+ the update orchestration. Unlike the create-time writer (sub-unit 1, which
projects EVERY managed field), the update path routes only the fields present
in the patch, and properties.json is a READ-MODIFY-WRITE (a patch touching only
`title` must preserve pronouns/aliases/firstMessage/talkativeness). The unmanaged
remainder flows to the slim _update (skipped when empty — a managed-only update
does NOT bump the slim row's updatedAt).
FIXTURE: built by running v4's REAL repos.characters.create(character) so the
fixture holds a fully-formed character + vault (slim row, mount point, 7 folders,
the managed files, one systemPrompt, one scenario). Both the oracle and the Rust
port operate on a COPY of this SAME baked fixture, so the minted ids/timestamps
match and only the update-created rows differ per side. The character id is read
back from the fixture (SELECT id FROM characters) — both sides see the same one.
OPS:
1. { identity, title, isFavorite } — markdown routing (identity.md), a
properties.json RMW (title changes; pronouns/aliases/firstMessage/
talkativeness preserved), and a DB-only field (isFavorite true->false ->
slim _update, updatedAt minted).
2. { systemPrompts: [replaced] } — managed-only; reproject Prompts/ (sweep the
old Default.md, write the new prompt), slim row untouched.
REINDEX: the oracle drives the REAL update (no QUILLTAP_JOB_CHILD), so
reindexSingleFile runs; the differential pins the link chunkCount and excludes
doc_mount_chunks (as for groups/projects/create).
NORMALIZATION: minted-values remap with ONE shared id-map across all six tables
(characters -> points -> folders -> files -> documents -> links, natural-key
order). FKs verify by relationship; timestamps -> <ts>; chunkCount -> <cc>. The
DB-field change (isFavorite) and every vault file's content diff EXACTLY.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key (the same one folders/tags/
wardrobe use). NOT the real Friday pepper; safe to commit.

character.name: Evangeline
character.identity: A wandering scholar of forgotten tongues.
character.title: the scholar

systemPrompts.name: Default
systemPrompts.content: You are Evangeline, a scholar of forgotten tongues.

scenarios.title: The Dim Tavern
scenarios.content: A dim tavern at dusk; rain against the panes.

patch.identity: A reformed scholar, now a careful spy.
patch.title: the spy

systemPrompts.name: Spy Mode
systemPrompts.content: You are Evangeline operating under cover. Be terse.
